hypothesis: Replacing the random path-point selection in NN-BAX with uncertainty-guided acquisition using an ensemble of equivariant neural surrogates to quantify predictive uncertainty will reduce the total number of expensive energy/force evaluations required to converge the NEB path by at least 2× across LJ7 and LJ38 transitions and small DFT-reliant systems, while preserving barrier heights and saddle-point coordinates within 1% of the ground truth.
